insight: Protect's FastMatcher (used for `require name`, etc.) does NOT treat `*X*` as a contains match. With a leading `*` it strips only that one and the pattern becomes `endsWith("X*")`, which almost never matches. The actual contains syntax is a bare word with no asterisks (e.g. `require name Key`). Quoted `"X"` is equals, `X*` is startsWith, `*X` is endsWith. The class docstring is misleading on this.

`Debug: [scan]` alone prints "Checking rule <name>" for every rule whose material matched, but does NOT print why a rule was rejected by `require`/`ignore` filters (e.g. require name pattern mismatch). To see filter rejection reasons, set `Debug: [scan, operator]` in settings.yml. Users (and bots) frequently mistake the "Checking rule" line as proof the rule fully ran.
